news.headline: Corelight reduces SIEM ingest by 80% without compromising security fidelity, enhancing storage cost savings and threat investigations.
news.summary: Corelight, a leading provider of network detection and response (NDR) solutions, revealed on February 18, 2025, in San Francisco, their latest data aggregation feature. This new capability aims to reduce storage costs while also enhancing the speed of threat hunting and forensic investigations. Corelight has seen steady growth, becoming a prominent player in the industry. This innovation will significantly impact operational efficiency and effectiveness, catering to the increasing demand for advanced security measures.

news.headline: Nike and SKIMS collaborate to launch NikeSKIMS, disrupting the fitness and activewear industry. Feb. 18, 2025.
news.summary: Two renowned companies have come together to revolutionize the worldwide fitness and activewear sector. Nike, Inc. (NYSE: NKE) and SKIMS have collaborated to introduce NikeSKIMS, marking a significant partnership in disrupting the industry. The joint venture aims to combine the strengths of both brands to create innovative offerings catering to fitness enthusiasts. The announcement was made in Beaverton, Ore., and Los Angeles on Feb. 18, 2025. This move is expected to shake up the market and lead to exciting developments in the field of athletic apparel.

news.headline: ECB's Cipollone: Risks to growth are seriously skewed to the downside.
US NAHB Housing Market Index Actual 42 (Forecast 46, Previous 47)
ECB's Cipollone: The output gap is likely to stay open.
Skyryse is named a finalist for the prestigious Collier Trophy for its innovative SkyOS, a universal operating system for flight.
news.summary: The company based in El Segundo, California, announced on February 18, 2025, that Skyryse®, the developer of SkyOS™, the inaugural universal operating system for flight, has been recognized as a contender for the esteemed Collier Trophy. This accolade is the highest honor in the field of aerospace, presented each year by the National Aeronautic Association.

news.headline: Vaycaychella (OTC: VAYK) ensures no dilution from $1M acquisition, with shares untouchable for 24 months unless over $0.005 PPS.
news.summary: Vaycaychella, Inc. (OTC Pink: VAYK) based in Atlanta, announced on February 18, 2025, that their common shares will remain unaffected by dilution following the acquisition of a company valued at $1 million. The company assured that there will be no dilution for at least 24 months after the acquisition, with the share price expected to stay at or above $0.005.

news.headline: Former VP Kamala Harris and Nuno Sebastiao to host Fireside Chat at HumanX AI conference, aiming to advance AI for a better future.
news.summary: In New York, the prestigious AI conference of 2025, HumanX, has revealed its headliners for the event. Former Vice President Kamala Harris and Nuno Sebastiao, Feedzai's Chairman, will be the main speakers at the conference. They will engage in a fireside chat discussing the advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) and its capacity to create a just, safe, and all-encompassing future. During the session, Former Vice President Harris will articulate her perspective on the AI landscape, underlining the importance of guiding its growth responsibly. This marks a significant event in harnessing the potential of AI to benefit humanity.

news.headline: ECB's Cipollone: Uncertainty is holding back investment.
Travel Insured Int'l partners with Cognizant Classic in Palm Beaches as official insurer, launches "Travel Beyond the Green" for golf protection.
news.summary: In Glastonbury, Connecticut, on February 18, 2025, Travel Insured International (TII) revealed its support for the Cognizant Classic in The Palm as a sponsor. TII is a top trip protection provider in the U.S. and is under Crum & Forster's (C&F) Accident & Health (A&H) Division.

news.headline: Frisco EDC introduces first Venture Capitalist in-Residence (VCiR) role in the region, aiming to attract more investments.
news.summary: On February 18th, 2025, Jon Nordby was appointed as the inaugural Venture Capitalist in-Residence (VCiR) by the Frisco Economic Development Corporation (FEDC) in Frisco, Texas. In a pioneering move for the region, Nordby will assume this unique role as part of the FEDC's strategic initiatives. This marks the first time such a position has been created in the area, emphasizing the FEDC's commitment to fostering innovative business practices.

news.headline: Pet product company POOPH® Inc. forms G-P.O.O.P.H. foundation to advocate toxin-free household products, aiming to Get Poisons Out Of People's Homes.
news.summary: Promoting the use of household products without toxins, a company called POOPH® Inc. has established a new Consumer Advocacy Organization named "G-P.O.O.P.H." This 501(c)(3) organization is dedicated to eliminating poisons from people's homes. The announcement was made in Huntington, N.Y., on Feb. 18, 2025. This new organization aims to raise awareness and educate consumers about the importance of toxin-free products. Many consumers are joining the cause as they are fed up with the presence of harmful chemicals in household items.

news.headline: Allspring introduces 3 active large-cap equity strategies to Remi platform, enhancing SMA options. #Allspring #investment #SMAs
news.summary: In Charlotte, North Carolina on February 18, 2025, Allspring Global Investments revealed the incorporation of three newly added actively managed large-cap equity strategies to its Remi platform. This platform specializes in customized manager-traded separately managed accounts (SMAs) and direct indexing services.

news.headline: Cipollone emphasizes need to consider QT when deciding on rate cuts: ECB statement.
SingularityNET, Mind Network introduce encryption for AI agents Homomorphic encryption could be crucial for privacy in decentralized AI. CEO Ben Goertzel explains.
news.summary: Ben Goertzel, who serves as the CEO of SingularityNET and ASI, predicts that fully homomorphic encryption could emerge as a vital element in privacy-focused, decentralized AI systems. This encryption method allows for data to be processed in an encrypted format without decryption, potentially revolutionizing the safeguarding of sensitive information in AI applications. This advancement may play a significant role in enhancing security and privacy in AI systems, as data remains encrypted throughout processing, reducing the risk of exposure.

news.headline: Tech firms are postponing IPO plans due to Trump's tariff announcements causing market instability. Years of in waiting.
news.summary: Several technology firms are reconsidering their decision to go public due to the market volatility and uncertainty stemming from the recent tariff announcements and policy changes by the Trump administration. This has resulted in a significant delay or withdrawal of listing plans. The exact number of companies affected by this decision is still unclear, but sources indicate a noticeable impact on the tech industry. Such actions highlight the challenges faced by businesses in navigating the current economic climate and adapting to rapidly changing trade policies.

news.headline: Arizona Diamondbacks partner with Aiper as Official Robotic Pool Cleaner, highlighting world-class cordless robotic pool cleaning products.
news.summary: The Arizona Diamondbacks revealed on February 18, 2025, a long-term collaboration with Aiper, designating them as the team's Official Robotic Pool Cleaner. Aiper, a top player in cordless robotic pool cleaners worldwide, will introduce their cutting-edge products to enhance pool maintenance for the Diamondbacks.

news.headline: OPAC Global developing AI systems w/ OpenAI for data collection & infrastructure; focus on security & resilience.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, in London, OPAC Global announced its efforts in creating AI-powered systems utilizing OpenAI technologies to improve security and infrastructure resilience. The company is focused on developing tools that can gather, assess, and organize data. This initiative aims to enhance security measures and strengthen infrastructure.

news.headline: NIICA awards Maddison Spaulding Memorial Scholarships to 5 Registered Apprentices in semiconductor and advanced manufacturing - Feb 18, 2025.
news.summary: A scholarship has been created to commemorate a committed apprentice, providing support for Registered Apprentices (RAs) as they enter the fields of semiconductor and advanced manufacturing. The initiative, led by the National Institute for Industry and Career Advancement™ (NIICA), aims to assist RAs in pursuing careers in these industries. This scholarship fund has allocated a total of (specific number) for eligible applicants, with the application process opening on February 18, 2025. The establishment of this scholarship signifies the importance of honoring the legacy of skilled apprentices and facilitating their professional development within the semiconductor and advanced manufacturing sectors.

news.headline: Over 500 Teamsters drivers at 10 Roads Express strike for fair contracts against USPS contractor.
news.summary: More than 500 Teamsters drivers working for 10 Roads Express, a major private contractor for the United States Postal Service (USPS), are currently on strike across the nation demanding fair contracts. The drivers were forced onto picket lines as they seek better terms.

news.headline: BreAnna McCready joins StoneEagle as Senior VP of Sales, leading its F&I solutions for the automotive industry.
news.summary: In Richardson, Texas, on February 18, 2025, StoneEagle®, a renowned provider of F&I solutions for the automotive sector for more than 35 years and a leading repair documentation service, has excitedly shared the news of augmenting its leadership squad by bringing aboard Breanna McCready.

news.headline: Endovascular Engineering secures $42 million in funding for clot removal tech to treat Pulmonary Embolism.
news.summary: A company based in Menlo Park, California, called Endovascular Engineering, Inc. (E2), recently announced that it has successfully raised $42 million in a Series B financing round. The funding exceeded expectations, indicating high interest in the company's innovative clot removal technology for venous thromboembolism (VTE). The financing round was co-led by 415 Capital, demonstrating strong support from investors. This investment will enable E2 to further develop and enhance its advanced platform for treating VTE.

news.headline: Ukraine’s President Zelenskiy: I plan to visit Saudi Arabia in March.
Ukraine’s President Zelenskiy: My visit to Saudi Arabia has been postponed until March 10th I am waiting for the US delegation in Kyiv.
Ukraine’s President Zelenskiy I won't go to Saudi Arabia.
Dallas-Plano-Irving home prices in January 2025 unchanged from the previous year, as per First American Data & Analytics HPI report.
news.summary: In Santa Ana, California, First American Data & Analytics, a division of First American Financial Corporation (NYSE: FAF), has published the January 2025 Home Price Index (HPI) report. This report provides property-centric data, risk management, and valuation solutions. It offers insights on home price changes nationally, by state, and in metropolitan areas (Core-Based Statistical Areas), including diverse price tiers. The report is updated regularly with data close to real-time, offering valuable information for market analysis.

news.headline: AE Industrial invests in UBS to create a comprehensive commercial HVAC services platform in the Northeast U.S.
news.summary: A private equity firm, AE Industrial Partners, LP, has collaborated with United Building Solutions (UBS), a top HVAC service provider in the Northeast U.S., to establish a specialized platform for complex HVAC services in commercial buildings. This partnership aims to enhance HVAC offerings for clients. UBS has also teamed up with Total, expanding their capabilities to better serve the market. AE Industrial focuses on sectors like National Security, Aerospace, and Industrial Services.

news.headline: Barings acquires Artemis Real Estate Partners, an $11+ billion real estate firm, to enhance US market presence.
news.summary: Barings, a prominent investment manager, has recently disclosed its plans to acquire Artemis Real Estate Partners in a definitive agreement. Artemis is a major player in the real estate investment sector with assets totaling over $11 billion. The move is geared towards bolstering Barings' position within the U.S. real estate market. By merging their unique investment strengths and knowledge, this acquisition aims to propel long-term growth for the platform. The deal is anticipated to be finalized soon.

news.headline: KKR partners with German energy service provider EGC, keeping founding family as stakeholders; Michael Lowak to chair.
news.summary: In Frankfurt, Germany, KKR, a prominent global investment firm, has revealed plans of forming a strategic partnership with EGC, an energy service provider situated in Düsseldorf, Germany. Additionally, the partnership includes ITG, an engineering service provider. The founding family and existing shareholders will preserve a portion of ownership in the company, continuing their roles within the management team. Michael Lowak, the former CEO of GETEC Group in Germany, will assume the position of Chairman within the group.

news.headline: Red Stone Equity Partners raised $1.43 billion in equity in 2024, highlighting growth strategy. Executive team strengthened.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, Red Stone Equity Partners LLC, a private real estate finance and investment firm focused on affordable multifamily fund management, revealed that 2024 was its most successful year to date. This announcement signifies a significant milestone for the company, showcasing impressive growth and accomplishments.

news.headline: E-Banking market valued at $9.3 trillion in 2023, set to reach $16.6 trillion by 2033, growing at CAGR of 5.6%.
news.summary: The report by Allied Market Research focuses on the E-Banking Market, stating it was valued at $9.3 trillion in 2023 and projected to reach $16.6 trillion by 2033, with a CAGR of 5.6% from 2024 to 2033. Key drivers of growth include technological advancements, digital transformation, internet and smartphone penetration, data analytics, customer insights, and e-commerce growth. However, challenges like device compatibility, security issues, privacy concerns, innovative banking services, and corporate investors may restrain market growth. The report covers details on service types, applications, end-users, forecast period from 2024 to 2033, with a 168-page analysis available.

news.headline: PICPA pushes for new bill to update CPA licensure laws in Pennsylvania, introducing an additional pathway with bipartisan backing.
news.summary: Philadelphia-based organization PICPA is supporting a new bill that proposes an alternative way to become a CPA in Pennsylvania. The bill has received bipartisan backing and aims to provide aspiring accountants with an additional route to obtaining their CPA license in the state.

news.headline: AKC launches "Everything We Do, We Do for Dogs" campaign celebrating dog-owner bond in Feb 2025.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, in New York City, the American Kennel Club (AKC®) unveiled its new initiative, "Everything We Do, We Do for Dogs." As the leading not-for-profit purebred dog registry globally, AKC aims to bolster brand recognition and advocacy for canines through this campaign. The organization collaborated with partners to create this brand awareness effort, highlighting its commitment to serving dogs.

news.headline: Net Health's annual client conference in Pittsburg focuses on AI, Policy, & Innovation in healthcare tech on Feb. 18, 2025. #NetHealthNEXT
news.summary: The second annual event is set to gather industry experts for practical training and valuable insights on enhancing patient care. Taking place on February 18, 2025 in Pittsburgh, the event will be hosted by Net Health, a reputable provider of specialized software solutions for healthcare providers. The event aims to facilitate collaboration and knowledge-sharing among professionals in the field.

news.headline: Veritas Veterinary Partners opens two new facilities in California, specializing in veterinary care.
news.summary: Two veterinary facilities, Veterinary Surgical Specialists of Orange County and Pet Emergency and Specialty Center of Marin, are set to provide specialized veterinary care in Tustin, California, and San Rafael, California, respectively. Veritas Veterinary Partners announced this expansion on February 18, 2025. Veritas is rapidly growing in the specialty veterinary care sector and aims to offer quality services in these locations.

news.headline: Kroll's Opinions Practice ranked No. 1 for fairness opinions for over 20 years globally, as per LSEG's report.
news.summary: For more than 20 years, Kroll has held the top spot in LSEG's fairness opinion rankings, a global recognition of its prestigious status. The announcement was made today in New York, with the independent provider of financial and risk advisory solutions solidifying its position as the leading entity in the industry. Kroll's No. 1 ranking stems from its unmatched total number of accomplishments in this realm.

news.headline: Pillar Biosciences & Hamilton Co. partner to automate rapid NGS testing. Strategic agreement announced on Feb. 18, 2025.
news.summary: Pillar Biosciences, Inc. in NATICK, Mass., on Feb. 18, 2025, announced a collaboration to enhance the automation of Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) testing. The agreement involves a partnership for NGS automation application development with a focus on accelerating testing processes. Pillar Biosciences, known for its expertise in Decision Medicine™, is aiming to boost efficiency through this strategic alliance. The joint effort aims to streamline NGS testing procedures, potentially improving turnaround times and scalability in the process.

news.headline: 47 Full Sail alumni credited on 18 Oscar-nominated projects at the 97th Academy Awards, with 2 graduates receiving nominations.
news.summary: In Winter Park, Florida, Full Sail University celebrates the nomination of 47 alumni for 18 projects across 21 categories at the 97th Annual Academy Awards. Among them, two graduates are up for their own Oscars.

news.headline: Lung Cancer Research Foundation partners with Israel Cancer Research Fund to test new treatment for KRAS-mutant cancer.
news.summary: Partnerships have been established for a research initiative aiming to test a novel treatment for KRAS-mutant cancer, currently in development at Hebrew University. The collaboration involves the Lung Cancer Research Foundation (LCRF) and Israel Cancer Research Fund (ICRF). The project is set to advance innovative cancer treatment approaches. The announcement was made in New York on February 18, 2025. This partnership underscores the importance of joint efforts in advancing cancer research and potentially improving outcomes for patients.

news.headline: San Juan Basin Royalty Trust (NYSE: SJT) will not declare a cash distribution for February 2025.
news.summary: Today, in Dallas, Argent Trust Company announced as the trustee of the San Juan Basin Royalty Trust (NYSE: SJT), that there will be no monthly cash distribution declared to holders of the trust's Units of beneficial interest. The Trustee made this decision in light of current financial circumstances.

news.headline: BHC Global acquires majority interest in POWERCONNECT.AI, further AI customer experience in utility & energy; BHC: 25-year consultancy.
news.summary: In Tampa Bay, Florida on February 18, 2025, POWERCONNECT.AI, a groundbreaking firm specializing in AI Customer Experience, announced the sale of a majority stake to BHC Global, a prominent digital innovation consultancy with 25 years of experience. The acquisition enables BHC Global to offer consulting, project management, and various other services to clients in need of AI solutions.

news.headline: Mom's Meals® exceeds 2024 Hunger Challenge target in fight against nutrition insecurity. Annual report shows strong commitments.
news.summary: Mom's Meals, a prominent provider of holistic food as medicine solutions based in Ankeny, Iowa, released its annual corporate social responsibility report on February 18, 2025. The report showcased the company's substantial efforts in combating nutrition insecurity throughout the year. Mom's Meals capped off the year with noteworthy contributions, with the total number amounting to a significant figure. The report emphasizes the company's ongoing commitment to addressing critical social issues and making a positive impact on communities.

news.headline: Costco Travel partners with Travelport to offer members diverse travel options through APIs and Travelport+, enhancing membership value.
news.summary: Costco Travel has announced its partnership with Travelport, a leading global technology company, to enhance travel options for its members. Utilizing Travelport APIs and the Travelport+ platform, Costco Travel will provide members with a wider selection of travel options and easier access to content from multiple suppliers. This collaboration, based in LANGLEY, United Kingdom, was officially announced on February 18, 2025. The initiative aims to streamline the travel booking process and offer Costco members a more diverse range of choices in their travel arrangements.

news.headline: Square 9 Softworks introduces Square 9 Shareworks: referral program offers rewards to customers for promoting Intelligent Information Management Platform.
news.summary: Square 9 Softworks has recently introduced Square 9 Shareworks, a customer referral initiative to expand the reach of their intelligent information management platform. This program enables customers to earn rewards for advocating the benefits of the Square 9 platform. The announcement was made on Feb. 18, 2025, in New Haven, Conn. The program aims to incentivize users to bring in new customers, boosting the platform's user base. It offers rewards to customers who successfully refer others to the platform, contributing to its growth.

news.headline: Marilyn Dale named NAWA Signature Member, honoring artistic achievements, organization supports women artists for 100+ years.
news.summary: Marilyn Dale, of Naperville, Illinois, was recently announced as a new Signature Member of The National Association of Women Artists (NAWA), a prestigious group that has been championing women artists for more than 100 years. This recognition celebrates Dale's accomplishments in the field of art.

news.headline: Dash and PEEPS® collaborate for special Easter edition waffle makers and egg cookers, adding magic to breakfast tables.
news.summary: The top waffle maker in America and the top non-chocolate Easter candy have revealed a collaboration to launch a special collection of mini waffle makers and egg cookers. This partnership between Dash and PEEPS® Brand introduces a limited-edition line featuring the famous Bunny and Chick characters in charming pink colors. The announcement was made on February 18, 2025 in New York.

news.headline: Soaak Technologies launched 'The Soaak Podcast', focusing on bio-optimization, wellness hacks, neuroscience. Tulsa, Feb 18, 2025.
news.summary: A new podcast called Soaak has officially launched, aiming to explore the future of bio-optimization, wellness hacks, and neuroscience for peak performance. This initiative comes from Soaak Technologies, Inc., a well-known health-tech company based in Tulsa, Oklahoma. The podcast is set to delve into clinically proven sound frequency techniques and cutting-edge research. The launch took place on February 18, 2025. For more information, visit https://www.soaak.com/.

news.headline: Knightvest Capital acquires SKYE of Turtle Creek in Dallas to renovate and compete with new constructions. - Feb. 18, 2025
news.summary: Knightvest Capital has revealed its intention to refurbish and reposition the multifamily community to better compete with the new constructions in the vicinity. The acquisition announced on February 18, 2025, includes the SKYE of Turtle Creek property. Knightvest Capital is a vertically integrated firm specializing in multifamily investments. The plan involves significant renovations and strategic positioning to enhance the community's appeal and market competitiveness. This move signifies an important step in Knightvest's investment strategy, showcasing its commitment to adapting to market demands and maintaining a competitive edge.

news.headline: Amex GBT receives provisional UK antitrust clearance for acquisition of CWT by Competition and Markets Authority.
news.summary: American Express Global Business Travel, a division of Global Business Travel Group, Inc. (NYSE: GBTG), a prominent travel, expense, and meetings & events software and services company, revealed that the UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has preliminarily determined no competition issues with GBT’s acquisition of CWT, a worldwide business travel and solutions provider.

news.headline: Leishen Energy Holdings reported fiscal year 2024 revenue of $69.07M, operating cash flow at $15.07M, gross profit at $16.03M, and net income at $7.99M. The company faced declines in revenue, profitability, and net income attributed to operational expenses and decreased domestic market sales. The company aims for international expansion, technology innovation, customer diversification, and operational efficiencies in fiscal year 2025.
news.summary: Leishen Energy Holding Co., Ltd. reported its fiscal year 2024 financial results showing notable growth in operating cash flow, which rose to USD $15.07 million (243% increase from fiscal year 2023). Total revenues decreased by 5.5% to USD $69.07 million, with gross profit at USD $16.03 million. Net income decreased by 31.3% to USD $7.99 million. The company's different segments had varying performance: Clean-Energy Equipment revenue declined by 14.6%, Digitalization and Integration Equipment remained stable, New Energy Sales grew by 11.3%, and Oil and Gas Engineering Technical Services revenue decreased by 8.4%. The CEO highlighted strengthening positions in new energy sales and international markets. The CFO emphasized cost optimization and strategic capital allocation. The company plans to focus on international expansion, technology and innovation, customer diversification, and operational efficiencies for fiscal year 2025. Operating expenses rose, with selling and marketing costs up by 164.6%, general and administrative expenses up by 7.7%, and research and development expenses up by 183.3%.
